items.title: Match distribution to measurement scale
items.avoid: Using MSE on classification labels or cross-entropy on unbounded real regression without checking error shape.
items.prefer: Bernoulli/Categorical + cross-entropy for class labels; Gaussian/MSE or robust losses for continuous targets; Poisson/Binomial for counts.
items.why: The loss is the negative log-likelihood of an assumed distribution; mismatch yields biased uncertainty and suboptimal fit.
items.example: Predicting click counts with linear regression can predict negatives; Poisson or Binomial log-likelihood keeps predictions in valid ranges.

items.title: Validate Poisson and Binomial assumptions
items.avoid: Fitting Poisson regression without comparing sample mean and variance on counts.
items.prefer: Plot or compute mean vs variance by group; use dispersion tests or alternative count models when Var ≫ Mean.
items.why: Wrong count models produce miscalibrated confidence and poor forecasts on tail events.
items.example: If monthly defects have mean 12 and variance 80, start with Negative Binomial rather than Poisson(12).

items.title: Check Gaussian fit for continuous features
items.avoid: Blindly applying Gaussian NB or Gaussian noise without inspecting residuals or per-class histograms.
items.prefer: Standardize or transform features; inspect Q-Q plots or skewness; use non-Gaussian likelihoods when needed.
items.why: Normal assumptions are convenient but fragile under outliers and skew.
items.example: Before GNB on pixel intensities 0–255, note truncation; scaled [0,1] features may still need logit or Beta models for best fit.

items.title: Keep PMF/PDF notation straight in code and docs
items.avoid: Calling `.pdf` on discrete counts or interpreting density at a point as a probability mass.
items.prefer: Use `.pmf` / `.logpmf` for Binomial/Poisson/Bernoulli; `.pdf` / `.logpdf` for Normal; cumulative `.cdf` for interval probabilities.
items.why: API misuse propagates into wrong evaluation metrics and simulation bugs.
items.example: In SciPy, `stats.poisson.pdf(3, mu=4)` does not exist—use `stats.poisson.pmf(3, mu=4)`.
